commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - Maintainers should decide whether the process-wide Active Memory lane is acceptable before merge; no automated repair is needed.; Cleared: The current diff only passes a lane string to an existing embedded run and adds a test; no dependency, workflow, secret, install, or package-resolution surface is changed. (sha=630953d9d5697b7e56b06a927cdefbdfdd4310fe)
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a dedicated `active-memory` embedded-run lane for Active Memory recall and a regression test asserting the lane is passed from the `before_prompt_build` hook.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +14. Total +16 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the linked report has live timeout logs, and current main calls the embedded recall without `lane` while omitted embedded lanes resolve to `main`. I did not rerun a live gateway reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - Maintainers should decide whether the process-wide Active Memory lane is acceptable before merge; no automated repair is needed.

target.title: fix #79026: active-memory recall subagent can deadlock on the main lane inside before_prompt_build
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Active Memory recall subagents could run on the same main lane as the parent prompt-build turn, which could self-block the parent session during `before_prompt_build`.
- Solution: Run Active Memory recall subagents on a dedicated `active-memory` lane.
- What changed: Updated `extensions/active-memory/index.ts` and added regression coverage in `extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`.
- What did NOT change: No channel transport behavior, memory tool semantics, provider defaults, or changelog content changed.

Fixes #79026

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Active Memory recall now starts and finishes on the dedicated `active-memory` lane during a real gateway webchat turn, instead of sharing the parent main lane during `before_prompt_build`.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw dev gateway from the current rebased branch with `pnpm gateway:dev`, `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, Active Memory enabled, Memory Core enabled, and an OpenAI-compatible loopback model endpoint for deterministic model replies.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  ```bash
  # Start a local OpenAI-compatible loopback model endpoint on 127.0.0.1:19076.
  OPENCLAW_HOME=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80255-evidence/gateway-home-mock-current     OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_PORT=19075     OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN=[redacted-token]     OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1     pnpm gateway:dev

  # Send a real webchat turn through the gateway.
  POST http://127.0.0.1:19075/v1/chat/completions
  x-openclaw-session-key: agent:main:direct:proof-80255-current

  pnpm exec vitest run extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts
  pnpm check:test-types
  ```
- **Evidence after fix:**
  ```text
OpenClaw dev gateway proof for PR #80255
Environment: isolated pnpm gateway:dev on local loopback, OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1, active-memory enabled, memory-core enabled
Model boundary: local OpenAI-compatible loopback provider returned deterministic responses
Session: agent:main:direct:proof-80255-current via POST /v1/chat/completions
HTTP /v1/chat/completions status=200 elapsedMs=39228
assistant_reply_excerpt= ACTIVE_MEMORY_PROOF_OK
2026-05-29T20:06:24.886+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, active-memory, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 8.1s)
2026-05-29T20:06:24.894+08:00 [gateway] ready
2026-05-29T20:07:30.769+08:00 [plugins] active-memory: agent=main session=agent:main:direct:proof-80255-current activeProvider=openai activeModel=loopback-chat start timeoutMs=30000 queryChars=66 searchQueryChars=66
2026-05-29T20:07:31.822+08:00 [plugins] active-memory: agent=main session=agent:main:direct:proof-80255-current activeProvider=openai activeModel=loopback-chat transcript=/media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-pr-80255-evidence/gateway-home-mock-current/.openclaw-dev/plugins/active-memory/transcripts/agents/main/active-memory-proof-80255/active-memory-mpqvnfte-63f9c581.jsonl
2026-05-29T20:07:31.825+08:00 [plugins] active-memory: agent=main session=agent:main:direct:proof-80255-current activeProvider=openai activeModel=loopback-chat done status=no_relevant_memory elapsedMs=1058 summaryChars=0
  ```
- **Observed result after fix:** The gateway returned HTTP 200 with `ACTIVE_MEMORY_PROOF_OK`; the Active Memory recall pass logged `start` and `done status=no_relevant_memory` on the dedicated `active-memory` lane in about 1.1s, with no `before_prompt_build` timeout and no main-lane self-block.
- **What was not tested:** No external hosted model provider was used for the final proof; the model boundary used a local OpenAI-compatible loopback endpoint so the runtime/session/lane behavior could be verified without external credential or network flake.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test plus test type-check and real gateway proof.
- Target test file: `extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: `runs recall on a dedicated active-memory lane` asserts the Active Memory embedded recall call passes `lane: "active-memory"`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: It pins the exact call-site contract that prevents prompt-build recall from sharing the parent main lane.

Validation run:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /media/vdc/code/ai/aispace/openclaw-worktrees/pr-80255
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  127 passed (127)
Duration  44.84s (transform 20.93s, setup 937ms, import 25.20s, tests 18.45s, environment 0ms)
$ pnpm tsgo:test
$ pnpm tsgo:core:test && pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo
$ node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.extensions.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/extensions-test.tsbuildinfo
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: The Active Memory recall subagent did not specify a plugin-owned lane, so it inherited/defaulted into the same lane family as the parent session work during prompt-build recall.
- Missing detection / guardrail: There was no regression test asserting that Active Memory recall uses a dedicated lane.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
